name: Composite Higgs
summary: The Higgs is a pseudo-Nambu-Goldstone boson of a spontaneously broken global symmetry of a new strongly coupled sector. The breaking pattern (e.g., SO(5) -> SO(4) in the Minimal Composite Higgs Model, MCHM5) protects m_H from quadratic divergences below the compositeness scale Lambda ~ few TeV.

addresses_puzzles.note: Quadratic sensitivity is cut off at the compositeness scale; remaining tuning between v and f ~ 1 TeV is currently a few percent. Stronger LHC bounds on top partners and W/Z scattering are squeezing the natural region.
Composite vector-like top partners mix with SM quarks; the same mixing that lowers M_top can shift V_us by O(0.1%).

touches_benchmarks.note: Higgs couplings deviate from SM by O(v^2/f^2); current measurements require v/f < 0.3-0.4.
Heavy resonances contribute to S, T parameters; viable parameter space requires custodial symmetry.

new_symmetries: global SO(5) (or larger) spontaneously broken to SO(4)
introduces.note: Form factor parameters, top-partner mass, and small explicit-breaking terms.

history: Kaplan-Georgi (1984) original construction; Agashe-Contino-Pomarol (2005) MCHM5 holographic realization. Active LHC search target via top partners and resonance-pair production.

references.citation: Kaplan and Georgi, 'SU(2) x U(1) breaking by vacuum misalignment', Phys. Lett. B 136, 183 (1984)
Agashe, Contino, Pomarol, 'The minimal composite Higgs model', Nucl. Phys. B 719, 165 (2005)
